author: sriramk
text:
The 
@dwarkesh_sp
 and Jensen podcast is a great listen. 

 it illustrates the schism in a lot of industry debates around “AGI” and the concept of AI timelines. 

Both parties represent a different school of thought and this is a great distillation of a lot of offline debates.

author: sriramk
text:
Having your computer run overnight on a task and then checking on it in the morning is one of the best feelings in tech. 

When I was a kid it was doing a multi part download or a slow running task. 

Now it is to run a harness / agent loop / something GPU bound overnight.

author: sriramk
text:
Eliezer -  don't want  rehash so many earlier discussions on FOOM/takeoff scenarios.

At the crux of this, the fears around open source and exaggerated focus on safety ("it's valid to think of blowing up data centers!") lead to a bunch of policy mistakes.

author: sriramk
text:

@DavidSacks

author: sriramk
text:
Disagree with this recent trend attributing human emotions and motivations to LLMs (“a reward”). This leads us down the path of doomerism and fear over AI. 

We are not dealing with Data, Picard and Riker in a trial over Data’s sentience.

we’re going to have a future of apps ( screens + data + CRUD) generated on the fly by models.

author: sriramk
text:
Today is a day we have been working towards for six months. We are announcing America’s AI action plan putting us on the road to continued AI dominance. 

The three core themes:
- Accelerate AI innovation
- Build American AI infrastructure 
- Lead in international AI
